message:
feat(layout): add node-aware orthogonal edge routing (bd-2nw.14)

Enhance edge routing to detect and avoid intermediate node bounding
boxes. Previously, edges routed through midpoints without considering
other nodes, causing visual overlap. Now:

- route_edge_points_with_obstacles() checks if midpoint segments
  intersect any node bounding box
- find_obstacle_nudge_x/y() compute nudged coordinates that route
  around obstacles with 8px margin
- build_edge_paths_with_orientation() passes all non-endpoint nodes
  as obstacles to the router
- 3 new tests verify obstacle avoidance, backward compatibility
  with empty obstacles, and horizontal layout avoidance

Full A* visibility-graph pathfinding deferred to follow-up bead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2nw.11","title":"Extract full canvas rendering from FrankenTUI canvas.rs","description":"# Full Canvas Rendering Extraction

Complete extraction of pixel-level canvas rendering from FrankenTUI's canvas.rs (1,651 lines) for fm-render-canvas.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs
- Lines: 1,651
- Purpose: Pixel-perfect canvas rendering for web/WASM

## Current State

fm-render-canvas has basic rendering (~2,200 lines) but needs:
- Full shape rasterization
- Anti-aliased edge drawing
- Text rendering with font metrics
- Layer compositing
- Viewport management

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Shape Rasterization
- Arc and circle drawing (Bresenham)
- Ellipse drawing
- Polygon filling
- Rounded rectangle corners
- All node shape variants

### 2. Edge Rendering
- Line drawing with thickness
- Anti-aliased lines
- Bezier curve approximation
- Arrow head rendering
- Dashed/dotted patterns

### 3. Text Rendering
- Glyph positioning
- Font metrics integration
- Text baseline alignment
- Word wrapping on canvas
- Emoji/Unicode support

### 4. Compositing
- Layer management
- Alpha blending
- Clipping regions
- Dirty rectangle tracking

### 5. Viewport System
- Pan and zoom
- Fit-to-content
- Scroll handling
- High-DPI support

### 6. Performance Features
- Dirty region optimization
- Offscreen buffer caching
- Progressive rendering
- Web Worker offloading

## Canvas API Integration

The reference uses:
- 2D canvas context methods
- Path2D for complex shapes
- ImageData for pixel access
- requestAnimationFrame for animation

fm-render-canvas needs:
- Canvas2dContext trait abstraction
- MockCanvas for testing
- web-sys integration (behind feature flag)

## WASM Considerations

- Memory management for large diagrams
- SharedArrayBuffer for workers
- WebGPU fallback path
- Mobile touch handling

## Acceptance Criteria

- All shapes from reference rendered
- Anti-aliased edges working
- Text rendering accurate
- Viewport interaction working
- Performance: 60fps for 200-node diagrams
- WASM demo rendering correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:11.678931909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:25:14.230757427Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:25:14.230075716Z","close_reason":"Canvas rendering extracted into fm-render-canvas with context.rs (15KB, Canvas2dContext trait + MockCanvas2dContext), renderer.rs (31KB, Canvas2dRenderer), shapes.rs (14KB, shape drawing primitives), viewport.rs (7.5KB, viewport/transform management). Full trait-based abstraction for WASM/web Canvas2D rendering.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","extraction","render","wasm"],"comments":[{"id":73,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"},{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Canvas primitive drawing (rect, circle, path, text)
2. Coordinate transformation correctness
3. Color handling (RGB, HSL, named colors)
4. Font metrics approximation accuracy

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram render to mock canvas
2. Pixel-accurate comparison with golden bitmaps
3. HiDPI/scaling correctness
4. Clip region handling

### Cross-Platform Tests
1. Native canvas (if implemented)
2. WASM canvas via headless browser
3. OffscreenCanvas support

### Visual Regression
Generate PNG output and compare with golden images:
- Threshold: <1% pixel difference
- Per-diagram-type golden files

### Logging Requirements
- Draw call trace for debugging
- Render timing breakdown
- Canvas state stack trace","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.12","title":"Implement adversarial input handling and security hardening","description":"# Adversarial Input Handling and Security Hardening (Parity and Shared Hardening)

Goal:
- Enforce robust security and bounded runtime behavior for untrusted inputs while preserving parity expectations.

Scope:
- Integrate shared security corpus and SVG sanitization policy.
- Validate parser, layout, and render behavior against adversarial and malformed inputs.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for limits, sanitizer decisions, and threat classification.
- Integration tests for parser to layout to render hardening paths.
- E2E security sweeps with deterministic verdicts and reproducible artifacts.
- Structured logs must include threat class, trigger, sanitizer action, budget usage, fallback path, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:31.976024788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.266989287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hardening","parser","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.790885694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.427788918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2hq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.306699631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.701233747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Adversarial Input Corpus
1. Extremely long node IDs (>10K chars)
2. Deeply nested structures (>100 levels)
3. Massive edge counts (>100K edges)
4. Malformed Unicode sequences
5. Control characters in labels
6. Escape sequence injection attempts

### Security Tests
1. No stack overflow on recursive input
2. Memory bounded for any input size
3. No DoS via pathological input
4. XSS prevention in SVG output
5. No command injection in CLI

### Fuzz Testing
Use cargo-fuzz with sanitizers:
```bash
cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_parser -- -max_len=100000
```

### Hardening Validation
1. All panics converted to Result errors
2. Resource limits enforced at all entry points
3. Graceful degradation under attack

### Logging Requirements
- Security-relevant events logged
- Rate of rejected inputs tracked
- Suspicious pattern detection","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.13","title":"Complete DOT format parser with full Graphviz compatibility","description":"# Complete DOT Format Parser

Extend fm-parser's DOT parser to achieve high compatibility with Graphviz DOT format, enabling import of existing DOT diagrams.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/dot_parser.rs
- Lines: 1,592
- Purpose: Parse Graphviz DOT format into shared IR

## Current State

fm-parser has basic DOT support (~500 lines) but needs:
- Full attribute parsing
- Subgraph/cluster support
- Edge properties
- Record shapes
- HTML labels

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Graph Structure
- digraph (directed)
- graph (undirected)
- strict graphs (no multi-edges)
- subgraph and cluster

### 2. Node Attributes
- shape (box, circle, diamond, record, etc.)
- label (plain and HTML)
- style (solid, dashed, filled)
- color, fillcolor
- fontname, fontsize
- width, height
- pos (for importing positioned graphs)

### 3. Edge Attributes
- label
- style (solid, dashed, dotted)
- color
- arrowhead, arrowtail
- headlabel, taillabel
- constraint

### 4. Graph Attributes
- rankdir (TB, LR, BT, RL)
- bgcolor
- splines
- nodesep, ranksep
- compound

### 5. Special Features
- Record shapes (nested fields)
- HTML-like labels
- Edge ports (n, ne, e, se, s, sw, w, nw)
- ID quoting rules
- Comments (// and /* */)
- Preprocessor directives

### 6. Compatibility Levels

| Level | Features |
|-------|----------|
| Basic | digraph, nodes, edges |
| Standard | Attributes, clusters |
| Extended | Records, HTML labels |
| Full | Ports, preprocessor |

## Mapping to IR

```
DOT node → IrNode
DOT edge → IrEdge  
DOT subgraph → IrCluster
DOT attribute → IrNode.classes or style hints
```

## Testing

- Graphviz test suite compatibility
- Round-trip: DOT → IR → DOT
- Large real-world DOT files
- Edge cases from Graphviz docs

## Acceptance Criteria

- All common DOT features parsed
- Attribute mapping to IR complete
- Record shapes working
- HTML labels handled
- Test coverage: Graphviz examples
- Documentation of compatibility level","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:49.361263986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.421290930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dot","graphviz","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.13","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.879327938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":91,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:02Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### DOT Grammar Coverage
1. All node shapes (box, circle, diamond, etc.)
2. All edge styles (solid, dashed, dotted)
3. Subgraph/cluster syntax
4. Attribute lists on nodes/edges/graphs
5. Port connections
6. Record-based labels

### Graphviz Compatibility Tests
Use Graphviz test corpus:
1. Parse all Graphviz examples
2. Compare layout with reference output
3. Track compatibility percentage

### Integration Tests
1. DOT to IR conversion correctness
2. IR to layout to render pipeline
3. Round-trip: DOT -> IR -> DOT (where applicable)

### Logging Requirements
- Parse trace with DOT-specific tokens
- Attribute value extraction details
- Unsupported attribute warnings","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2nw.14","title":"Implement advanced edge routing with orthogonal and spline paths","description":"# Advanced Edge Routing

Implement sophisticated edge routing algorithms from FrankenTUI to support orthogonal routing, curved edges, and proper edge-node avoidance.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs
- Lines: ~800 (routing section)
- Purpose: High-quality edge paths

## Current State

fm-layout has basic edge routing but lacks:
- Orthogonal (right-angle) routing
- Node avoidance
- Edge-edge avoidance
- Port assignment
- Curved edge smoothing

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Orthogonal Router

**Algorithm**: Visibility graph + A* pathfinding

Steps:
1. Build obstacle grid (nodes as rectangles)
2. Compute visibility graph
3. Find shortest orthogonal path
4. Apply bend minimization
5. Assign edge channels

**Properties:**
- O(n²) visibility computation
- O(e log n) pathfinding per edge
- Guaranteed to find path if exists

### 2. Edge Channel Assignment

When multiple edges share a path segment:
1. Compute channel requirements
2. Assign channels to minimize crossings
3. Apply channel-specific offsets
4. Preserve edge ordering

### 3. Port Assignment

For nodes with multiple edges:
1. Group edges by direction
2. Assign port positions
3. Minimize edge crossings at ports
4. Handle multi-edge bundles

### 4. Curved Edge Smoothing

Convert orthogonal paths to smooth curves:
1. Identify corner points
2. Compute Bezier control points
3. Generate cubic Bezier segments
4. Preserve tangent continuity

### 5. Node Avoidance

Ensure edges don't cross through nodes:
1. Detect potential crossings
2. Route around obstacles
3. Minimize detour length
4. Handle nested clusters

### 6. Long Edge Handling

For edges spanning multiple layers:
1. Create dummy nodes at each layer
2. Route through dummies
3. Align dummy nodes for straightness
4. Handle wide nodes

## Rendering Integration

Edge paths output as:
```rust
pub enum EdgePath {
    Straight { from: Point, to: Point },
    Orthogonal { segments: Vec<(Point, Point)> },
    Bezier { segments: Vec<BezierCubic> },
}
```

## Performance Targets

| Graph Size | Routing Time |
|------------|--------------|
| 100 nodes | <50ms |
| 500 nodes | <200ms |
| 1000 nodes | <500ms |

## Acceptance Criteria

- Orthogonal routing working
- No edge-node intersections
- Port assignment minimizing crossings
- Curved edges option available
- Visual parity with FrankenTUI
- Performance within targets","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:07.733066831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.589949576Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edges","layout","routing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.788304429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":72,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"},{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Orthogonal Routing Tests
1. Channel assignment correctness
2. No edge crossings through nodes
3. Minimum bend count
4. Path length optimization

### Spline Path Tests
1. Bezier control point calculation
2. Smooth curve through waypoints
3. No self-intersecting curves
4. Consistent curvature

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram with mixed edge styles
2. Layout determinism with edge routing
3. Performance with many edges (>1000)

### Visual Regression
Golden output for edge routing scenarios:
- Dense graphs with many crossings
- Sparse graphs with long paths
- Hierarchical layouts

### Logging Requirements
- Routing algorithm trace
- Channel occupancy map
- Bend points selected","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:38Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2nw.15","title":"Build automated feature parity verification against FrankenTUI","description":"# Automated Feature Parity Verification against FrankenTUI

Goal:
- Generate objective parity evidence that combines capability metadata, E2E telemetry, and proof artifacts into release consumable reports.

Scope:
- Build feature matrix generation from shared capability sources and runtime evidence.
- Compare reference and FM outputs semantically and perceptually with explicit confidence tags.
- Publish machine readable and human readable parity dashboards.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for matrix generation, scoring rules, and threshold evaluation.
- Integration tests for ingestion of reference capture plus CLI and WASM artifacts.
- E2E parity runs with deterministic replay and triage outputs.
- Structured logs must include feature id, support state, reference verdict, FM verdict, delta type, confidence, artifact links, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:26.913410616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.757022782Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.776755747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.120837335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.208363086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.866478867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.952552130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.036757681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.913294434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.550949443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.793558913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.674886281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Feature Parity Matrix
Automated verification against FrankenTUI:
1. All diagram types parsed
2. All node shapes rendered
3. All edge styles supported
4. All styling options work

### Automated Comparison
```bash
# scripts/parity-check.sh
for sample in corpus/*.mmd; do
  frankentui_output=$(frankentui render $sample)
  frankenmermaid_output=$(fm-cli render $sample)
  compare_outputs $frankentui_output $frankenmermaid_output
done
```

### Coverage Tracking
1. Feature checklist with pass/fail status
2. Percentage coverage metric
3. Regression detection on coverage drop

### Logging Requirements
- Detailed comparison trace
- Difference categorization (visual, structural, semantic)
- Prioritized gap list","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.2","title":"Extract minimap rendering from mermaid_minimap.rs into fm-render-term","description":"# Minimap Rendering Extraction

Extract the complete minimap functionality from FrankenTUI's mermaid_minimap.rs (1,474 lines) into frankenmermaid's fm-render-term crate.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_minimap.rs
- Lines: 1,474
- Purpose: Overview visualization of large diagrams with viewport indicator

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Minimap Computation
- Proportional scaling to fit overview area
- Node density-based detail level selection
- Viewport rectangle calculation
- Pan/zoom coordinate mapping

### 2. Rendering Modes
- Terminal minimap (block characters)
- Simple minimap (ASCII only)
- Colored minimap (highlight regions)

### 3. Viewport Interaction
- Viewport position calculation
- Click-to-navigate coordinate mapping
- Viewport size indicator

### 4. Performance Features
- Lazy rendering (only visible regions)
- Cached minimap with dirty tracking
- Progressive detail rendering

## Implementation Notes

The FrankenTUI minimap uses:
- Downsampling algorithms for node representation
- Edge simplification (straight lines only)
- Color quantization for terminal output

Key adaptations needed:
1. Remove ftui-style dependencies
2. Integrate with fm-layout positioned nodes
3. Support multiple output targets (term, SVG, canvas)
4. Add streaming/progressive rendering

## Acceptance Criteria

- All minimap modes from reference implemented
- Unit tests for scaling/viewport math
- Integration with fm-render-term
- Visual regression tests
- Performance: <50ms for 1000-node diagrams","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:27.959621218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:24:48.828085908Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:24:48.827224627Z","close_reason":"Minimap rendering extracted into fm-render-term/src/minimap.rs (21KB). Includes MinimapConfig, MinimapResult, scaled overview rendering with viewport indicator and proportional positioning.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extraction","minimap","render"],"comments":[{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons.  {"id":"bd-2nw.3","title":"Complete Sugiyama coordinate assignment with Brandes-Köpf algorithm","description":"# Sugiyama Coordinate Assignment Completion

Implement the complete Brandes-Köpf coordinate assignment algorithm from FrankenTUI's mermaid_layout.rs to achieve pixel-perfect layout parity.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_layout.rs
- Lines: ~3,000 (coordinate assignment section)
- Algorithm: Brandes-Köpf (2001) with enhancements

## Current State

fm-layout has basic coordinate assignment but lacks:
- Proper block alignment
- Median positioning
- Compact horizontal assignment
- Type 1/Type 2 conflict resolution

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Vertical Alignment Phase
- Mark type-1 conflicts (where edges cross)
- Mark type-2 conflicts (long edges)
- Determine median alignment
- Handle multi-edge nodes

### 2. Horizontal Compaction Phase
- Block building (aligned node groups)
- Class separation computation
- Minimum width assignment
- Sink-based coordinate shifting

### 3. Four-Way Computation
- Upper-left alignment
- Upper-right alignment
- Lower-left alignment
- Lower-right alignment
- Final: median of four positions

### 4. Edge Straightening
- Long edge dummy alignment
- Port assignment
- Bend minimization

## Algorithm Properties (Alien-Artifact-Coding)

- Time complexity: O(n) where n = nodes + dummy nodes
- Space complexity: O(n)
- Determinism: Stable ordering guarantees identical output
- Proof: Each phase preserves layer assignment invariant

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI uses bidirectional sweep with:
1. LEFT_UP, RIGHT_UP, LEFT_DOWN, RIGHT_DOWN passes
2. Median of 4 positions for final coordinates
3. Compact representation via class graph

Critical: Preserve exact FrankenTUI behavior for determinism verification.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 4-way alignment implemented
- Visual parity with FrankenTUI reference
- Property tests for invariants
- Performance: <10ms for 100-node graphs
- Determinism proof (golden tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:44.540525239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:03:30.054442680Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:03:30.053728257Z","close_reason":"Implemented Brandes-Köpf 4-way coordinate assignment algorithm in fm-layout. Added vertical alignment (4 directions: UL, UR, LL, LR), horizontal compaction with block/class separation, and median combination. 6 new tests (95 total). All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","layout","sugiyama"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.693988459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":109,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Algorithm Correctness Tests
1. Vertical alignment phase preserves layer assignment
2. Block building creates valid aligned groups
3. Class separation computation is correct
4. Horizontal compaction minimizes width

### Four-Way Computation Tests
1. Upper-left alignment produces valid coordinates
2. Upper-right alignment is mirror of upper-left
3. Lower-left alignment respects reversed layers
4. Lower-right alignment combines both mirrors
5. Final coordinate is median of four results

### Property Tests (proptest)
1. All node coordinates are finite
2. No horizontal overlaps within layers
3. Layer gaps are consistent
4. Total width is minimal for given constraints

### Determinism Tests
1. Same graph = same coordinates (100 runs)
2. Cross-platform determinism (Linux/macOS/WASM)
3. Floating-point consistency

### Golden Tests
Reference outputs for:
- Simple DAG (5-10 nodes)
- Complex DAG with long edges
- DAG with clusters
- Wide flat graph

### Logging Requirements
- Per-phase coordinate dump
- Block structure trace
- Class assignment decisions","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:53Z"}]}
